_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Technical specialism involves understanding the direction of future technologies; delivering a model to support and maintain those future technologies and any databases that coexist in the current environment.
reference_document_element_description: Skill in translating technical data and AI concepts into accessible language and relatable examples for non-technical business audiences without sacrificing accuracy.
comments: DDaT skill intersects with WIDAI OP domain. OP-S-004 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: AI-S-004 (AI, cosine: 0.375), TF-A-004 (TF, cosine: 0.373).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 3.8/10 (raw 0.377). Shared vocabulary: [technical]. DDaT-only terms [coexist, current, databases, delivering, direction, environment] vs WIDAI-only terms [accessible, accuracy, ai, audiences, business, concepts] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.850 (P=0.850, R=0.850). Balanced P/R — the 28-word DDaT element and 21-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: contradiction-dominant (98.9%). Despite 'Intersects with' classification, the model detects semantic tension between "Technical specialism involves understanding the direction of future technologies" and "Skill in translating technical data and AI concepts into accessible language and". Indicates these concepts operate in adjacent but distinct professional domains — shared vocabulary, different application context.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.456 > STS 0.377 (gap +0.078). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [technical] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 7.3%. Shared tokens: [technical]. DDaT-only: [coexist, current, databases, delivering, direction, environment]. WIDAI-only: [accessible, accuracy, ai, audiences, business, concepts].

signals.detail: NLI contradiction 98.9% suggests semantic tension between the FDE and matched KSA. The competency domains may be adjacent but not functionally aligned.
